Benvenuti

A little of Castelforte, halfway up Silver Street.

Coviello is a family run restaurant and wine bar bringing authentic Italian food, coffee and fine wines to the middle of Durham. Everything is home cooked, the meat is sliced fresh to order, and the wine list is chosen by people who drink it.

Come for an espresso in the morning, a plate of pasta at lunch, or a bottle and a tagliere that stretches into the evening. The door is open from ten.

Read our story →

What we do

Three good reasons to come in.

Kitchen, cellar and coffee bar under one roof — plus a monthly tasting worth clearing a Sunday for.

Kitchen

Food & drinks

Neapolitan pizza, handmade ravioli, aubergine parmigiana and taglieri of cured meat sliced fresh — never pre-cut.

View the menu → Monthly

Wine tasting

One Sunday a month we open bottles worth talking about and walk you through every one of them.

How it works → Cellar

The wine list

Aglianico to Soave, by the glass, the carafe or the bottle. Three 75ml pours if you'd rather explore than commit.

See the list →

Sundays, monthly

Wine tasting evenings

Once a month we set the room for tasting. A guided flight, a board to go with it, and someone at the front who can tell you why the Aglianico from Campania tastes of cinnamon and cloves.

It's a good way to find a bottle you'd never have picked off a shelf. Dates go out on Instagram and Facebook first — they tend to fill.

Dates on Instagram →

At the table

Wine pairing, without the ceremony

Tell us what you're eating and we'll tell you what to drink with it. No script, no upsell — just the pairing that makes both the plate and the glass better.

Ask at the bar, or add a note when you book.

  • Tuesday – Saturday10:00 – 23:00
  • Sunday11:00 – 23:00
  • MondayClosed

24 Silver Street, Durham

A table is usually easier than you think.

Message us with a date and a number of people and we'll come straight back to you.

The menu

Taglieri — the platters

The ultimate Coviello offering. Served with homemade bread, olives, extra virgin olive oil and balsamic, crackers, chutney and cornichons.

Castelforte → Durham

Our story

A family business built on two things: quality, and the experience of sitting down to eat properly.

From the Italian hillsides of Castelforte — halfway between Rome and Naples — we bring you authentic Italian food, coffees and fine wines.

Daniel grew up in Castelforte, a village set back from a stretch of coastline most people never get round to visiting. He went into catering young, spent years managing the family business in Italy, and carried the whole of it with him when he moved to England.

He had always wanted a place of his own — a wine bar and restaurant where the cooking was the kind you'd get at home, and the wine list was chosen by someone who actually loved drinking it. Coviello is that place. Two things he knows well, put in one room on Silver Street.

Everything here follows from that. The tomato sauce is made in house. The ravioli are made in house. The meat for the taglieri is sliced fresh in front of you, never pre-cut, because it tastes different and anyone who's eaten it properly can tell. Once a month the room turns over to a tasting and the good bottles come out.

It's a small place, run by a family. That's rather the point.

Home cooked

Sauces, ravioli, gnocchi and parmigiana made on site, the way they're made at home rather than the way they're made at scale.

Sliced fresh

Every cured meat on the board is cut to order. No pre-cut, ever — it's the one rule nobody here will bend.

Chosen by hand

From Montepulciano to Pinot Nero, the list is short on purpose. Ask and we'll tell you exactly why each one is on it.

Coffee, wine, cocktails

Open from ten, all the way through

Coviello started as a coffee bar and never stopped being one. Espresso in the morning, lunch in the middle, and a spritz that turns into a bottle by the evening — the room changes character three times a day and you're welcome at any of them.
